epigraph.text: As democracy is perfected, the office represents, more and more closely, the inner soul of the people. We move toward a lofty ideal. On some great and glorious day the plain folks of the land will reach their heart's desire at last, and the White House will be adorned by a downright moron.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Gnome Sweet Schemes: Florida Man's Ceramic DUI Ruse Leaves Police Baffled
follyOfTheDay.summary:
In a display of logic that can only be described as distinctly Florida-adjacent, a 47-year-old Tampa resident recently conducted an elaborate experiment in vehicular evasion that relied entirely on the criminal sophistication of garden statuary. Upon his arrest at a DUI checkpoint, police discovered the vehicle to be populated by no fewer than 23 ceramic garden gnomes, each meticulously positioned in seats and at the wheel to create the illusion of autonomous operation.

The suspect, when questioned, offered no apologies. Instead, he expounded at length regarding the gnomes' alleged consciousness, their ability to sense police presence, and what he described as their 'collective responsibility' to navigate him to freedom. Officers, displaying the restraint for which law enforcement is renowned, carefully documented the scene while wrestling with the existential question of whether they were investigating a crime or an elaborate cry for professional intervention.

The gnome collection, now in police custody, remains silent on the matter—which, one might note, makes them more coherent than their owner.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: If you're going to evade a DUI checkpoint, at least make your decoys believable. Ceramic consistency is no substitute for cognitive function.
